docs.title: MHDTurbulence
docs.text: This repository contains a 3D MHD solver (MPI + OpenACC/OpenMP target/OpenMP). The current default test problem is the Kelvin–Helmholtz instability.

docs.title: How to copy the source code
docs.text:
After you login the server, copy the programs.

git clone git@github.com:cfcanaoj/MHDTurbulence MHDTurbulence
cd MHDTurbulence

docs.title: Compile
docs.text:
To run the code, first compile source on a GPU server. The OpenACC version (src_f90_acc_device) is the default and recommended setup.

cd src_f90_acc_device
make

The executable Simulation.x is created in ../exe directory. If you prefer another implementation, use the appropriate directory and codes listed below.

directory Language GPU/CPU Parallelization src_f90_acc_device Fortran GPU MPI OpenACC src_f90_omp_device Fortran GPU MPI OpenMP Target src_f90_omp_host Fortran CPU MPI OpenMP src_cpp_omp_device C++ GPU MPI OpenMP src_cpp_kokkos_device C++ GPU MPI Kokkos src_cpp_kokkos_host C++ CPU MPI Kokkos

For stable simulations, please use the OpenACC (src_f90_acc_device) or CPU (src_f90_omp_host) versions.

docs.title: Execution
docs.text:
Copy the batch script and submit the job. The script for Slurm is prepared.

cd ../exe
cp ../misc/sj_g00.sh .
sbatch sj_g00.sh

Batch scripts depend on your parallelization scheme and environment.

script GPU/CPU directory sj_g00.sh GPU src_f90_acc_device, src_f90_omp_device, src_cpp_omp_device, src_cpp_kokkos_device sj_xd.sh CPU src_f90_omp_host, src_cpp_kokkos_host

docs.title: Problem setup: Kelvin–Helmholtz instability
docs.text: The initial condition is defined in GenerateProblem in main.f90.

docs.title: Hydrodynamic variables
docs.text:
Uniform density and pressure with \(\rho = 1\) and \(p = 1\). A shear flow in the x-direction with two shear layers centered at \(y=\pm 0.5\):

  • \(\Delta v = 2.0\)
  • shear-layer width \(w = 0.05\)
\[ v_x(y)=\frac{\Delta v}{2}\left[\tanh\left(\frac{y+0.5}{w}\right)-\tanh\left(\frac{y-0.5}{w}\right)-1\right] \]

A small perturbation in the y-direction to seed KH roll-up (sinusoidal in x, localized around the layers):

  • amplitude \(10^{-2}\)
  • localization width \(\sigma = 0.2\)

[ v_y(x,y)=10^{-2}\sin(2\pi x)\left[e^{-(y+0.5)^2/\sigma^2}+e^{-(y-0.5)^2/\sigma^2}\right] ] [ v_z = 0 ]

Additionally, the code contains a random perturbation term for \(v_x\), but in the current setup it is disabled:

  • \(\mathrm{rrv}=0.0\times 10^{-2}\)
  • if enabled, the perturbation is
\[ v_x \leftarrow v_x + \Delta v\,\mathrm{rrv}\,(r-0.5), \qquad r\in[0,1) \]

docs.title: Magnetic field
docs.text:
In the current KH setup, the magnetic field is initialized to zero:

\[ \mathbf{B} = (0,0,0) \]

docs.title: Tracer / composition field
docs.text:
Xcomp(1,...) is initialized as a smooth step-like tracer associated with the two layers, useful for visualizing mixing.

\[ X_{\mathrm{comp}} = \frac{1}{2}\left[ \tanh\left(\frac{y+0.5}{w}\right) - \tanh\left(\frac{y-0.5}{w}\right) \right] \]

docs.title: Boundary conditions
docs.text:
Boundary conditions are applied in config.f90. In the default, reflection boundary is taken for y-direction and periodic boundart is taken for other directions.

  integer,parameter:: periodicb=1,reflection=2,outflow=3
  integer,parameter:: boundary_xin=periodicb , boundary_xout=periodicb
  integer,parameter:: boundary_yin=reflection, boundary_yout=reflection
  integer,parameter:: boundary_zin=periodicb , boundary_zout=periodicb

docs.title: Real-time analysis
docs.text:
RealTimeAnalysis in src_f90_acc_device/main.f90 evaluates bulk diagnostics during the run and writes them to t-prof.csv.

The routine computes the cell-volume integral over the local MPI domain,

\[ \Delta V_{ijk} = \left(x1a_{i+1}-x1a_i\right) \left(x2a_{j+1}-x2a_j\right) \left(x3a_{k+1}-x3a_k\right), \]

then reduces the following quantities over all MPI ranks:

\[ V = \sum_{ijk}\Delta V_{ijk}, \] \[ M = \sum_{ijk} X_{\mathrm{comp},ijk}\left(1-X_{\mathrm{comp},ijk}\right)\Delta V_{ijk}, \] \[ S_{v_y} = \sum_{ijk} v_{y,ijk}^2\,\Delta V_{ijk}. \]

The reported diagnostics are

\[ \mathrm{mix} = \frac{M}{V}, \] \[ \langle v_y^2 \rangle^{1/2} = \sqrt{\frac{S_{v_y}}{V}}. \]

Here, mix is a simple mixing indicator based on the tracer field. It is near zero in unmixed regions where \(X_{\mathrm{comp}}\) is close to 0 or 1, and becomes larger where the two layers are mixed.

The file t-prof.csv contains four columns:

  1. time
  2. mix
  3. sqrt(<v_y^2>)
  4. A exp(\Gamma t)

The fourth column is a reference exponential growth curve,

\[ A\exp(\Gamma t), \]

with the hard-coded parameters

  • \(A = 1.2\times 10^{-3}\)
  • \(\Gamma = 1.49\)

These values are used as a practical comparison metric for KH growth in this setup. The source comment notes that this is not a general analytic growth rate for finite-width shear layers, but an empirical reference value used for this problem.
